page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 1952 GB of RAM, providing 15.25 GB of memory per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieved a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 81,431.63, driven by strong performance in extended instructions and encryption tests. Multi-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench multi-core scores are also in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 6,507.69 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 298.49 GB/sec for large-block reads. However, single-core performance remains average, as shown by Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core metrics.

Given its massive memory footprint and dedicated compute resources, this instance represents a high-end hardware class designed for resource-dense workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs means storage-heavy or hardware-accelerated AI workloads must rely on network-attached resources or external accelerators, which may introduce latency. It is highly optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, such as Redis, where it achieves 106.54 million operations per second, as well as heavy database operations, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and large-scale data analytics.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs (64 physical cores) operating at 2.6 GHz, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance features a massive 1952 GB of RAM, yielding 15.25 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth of up to 298.49 GB/sec for large-block reads. While single-core performance is average, its high memory density and strong multi-threaded capabilities make it highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, complex data analytics, and enterprise database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM, built for enterprise databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated multi-core processing for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
1952 GB of RAM providing 15.25 GB of memory per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance in benchmarks
Strong memory bandwidth reaching 298.49 GB/sec for large-block reads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads
